title: Foreign Currency Reserves 2026 – Market Notice 9 February 2026
summary: The Bank of England has announced its first 2026 foreign exchange reserve financing operation: a benchmark 3-year US dollar bond. The operation will be managed by four joint lead managers (RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley). This issuance follows the Bank's September 2025 announcement to shift to two benchmark issuances annually under a structured programme with regular timing and high transparency.
key_points: Bank of England mandated RBC Capital Markets, BMO Capital Markets, HSBC Bank plc, and Morgan Stanley as Joint Lead Managers
Operation will be a benchmark 3-year US dollar bond
Execution expected in the near future, subject to market conditions
This is the first 2026 operation under the updated issuance programme announced September 2025
The updated programme targets two benchmark issuances annually with regular timetable and high transparency
BoE foreign exchange reserves are separate from Government reserves managed on Treasury's behalf
why_it_matters: The BoE's shift to a structured, transparent issuance programme for foreign exchange reserve financing reduces uncertainty for market participants. Regular benchmark operations allow for more predictable supply in USD bond markets and establish clearer communication channels between the central bank and primary dealers. The use of multiple joint lead managers spreads distribution and may improve price discovery for these reserve-related instruments.
market_structure_context: The announcement reflects a formalisation of how the Bank of England accesses capital markets for reserve management. The programme structure with designated joint lead managers and regular issuance timing represents a change from potentially ad-hoc issuance toward a more institutionalised market operation, consistent with practices of other central banks managing foreign exchange reserves.
notes: The published_at date (2026-03-27) differs from the notice date (09 February 2026), suggesting this may be a retrospective publication or content update. Forward-looking statements disclaimer present, standard for sovereign issuance documentation. Target market is ECP/Professionals only per FCA/ICMA stabilisation provisions. The page contains significant legal boilerplate typical of securities offerings; core factual content about the issuance operation is limited but clearly stated.
